page_header.label: HOW IT WORKS
page_header.title: The Anatomy of a Confidential Payment
page_header.description: A deep dive into the cryptographic and technical architecture that makes ZKIRA Pay secure and private.

items.title: CREATE
items.description: Sender deposits tokens into an on-chain escrow account. A unique payment link with embedded claim secret is generated. The escrow is time-locked with configurable expiry for automatic refunds.

items.title: SHARE
items.description: Payment link is sent via any channel—Telegram, email, QR code, or direct message. The link contains only the claim secret. No wallet addresses are ever exposed in transit.

items.title: CLAIM
items.description: Recipient opens the link and connects their wallet. Our gas relayer sponsors the claim transaction. Funds arrive at a stealth address. For maximum privacy, use the shielded pool for ZK-proven private withdrawals.

paragraphs: The ZKIRA Pay payment pipeline involves multiple components working together: sender wallet, escrow program, claim secret generation, gas relayer, stealth address derivation, and recipient wallet. Each step is cryptographically secured and operates without trusted intermediaries.

items.title: Stealth Addresses
items.description: Every payment generates a unique one-time address. Your main wallet address remains completely private while staying publicly shareable.

items.title: Escrow Model
items.description: No direct wallet-to-wallet transfers. All payments go through smart contract escrow, breaking the on-chain connection between sender and receiver.

items.title: Gas Relayer
items.description: Recipients don't need SOL to claim payments. Our gas relayer sponsors all claim transactions, removing another privacy leak.

items.title: Shielded Pool
items.description: Fixed-denomination deposits with zero-knowledge proof withdrawals. The shielded pool breaks all on-chain links between deposits and withdrawals using Groth16 ZK proofs verified on Solana.

items.title: Privacy Network
items.description: Tor hidden service and Nym mixnet integration hide your IP address from RPC nodes. Your network traffic is untraceable.

items.title: Timing Defenses
items.description: 6-hour soak time, Poisson-distributed batch withdrawals, and automated decoy deposits defeat timing analysis attacks.

sections.label: SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE
sections.title: Three-Layer Architecture
sections.subtitle: ZKIRA Pay is built as a modular system with clear separation between application, protocol, and network layers.

sections.label: THREAT MODEL
sections.title: What We Protect Against
sections.subtitle: Every attack vector we've engineered defenses for — and the adversaries we defeat.

items.title: Transaction Graph Analysis
items.description: Shielded pool ZK proofs mathematically break all on-chain links between deposits and withdrawals. No amount of blockchain analysis can reconstruct the transaction graph.

items.title: Timing Correlation
items.description: 6-hour minimum soak time, Poisson-distributed batch withdrawals (~1hr mean), and automated decoy deposits every 30±10 minutes destroy all timing patterns.

items.title: Amount Correlation
items.description: Fixed 10 USDC denomination means every deposit looks identical. There's nothing to correlate — all transactions are the same size.

items.title: IP Fingerprinting
items.description: Tor hidden service routes all RPC traffic through .onion addresses. Nym mixnet provides browser-level packet mixing. Your IP never touches Solana.

items.title: Sender-Recipient Linking
items.description: ECDH stealth addresses generate unique one-time addresses for every payment. Even scanning the entire blockchain reveals nothing about who paid whom.

items.title: Metadata Leaks
items.description: Zero logs, zero analytics, blind relaying. The relayer only checks programId — it never inspects transaction data. No metadata exists to leak.
Resistance against Arkham Intelligence, Chainalysis, and Solscan. Commercial blockchain forensics cannot trace shielded pool transactions.
Resistance against nation-state adversaries with global network monitoring. The binding constraint is anonymity set size, not cryptography.

items.title: Unbreakable
items.description: The cryptographic primitives (ECDH, Groth16, Poseidon) cannot be broken by any known computation. The math is provably secure.

sections.title: Ready to try it?
sections.description: Experience confidential payments firsthand. Create your first payment link and see how simple privacy can be.
